content: I'm so frustrated with my knee injury. I've been out of commission for weeks and I'm itching to get back to my normal routine.
I'm sorry to hear that. What happened to your knee?
I twisted it while playing tennis. I've been playing for years, but I guess I'm not as invincible as I used to be.
Ouch, that sounds painful. Are you seeing a doctor or physical therapist to help with your recovery?

Yeah, I'm seeing a physical therapist twice a week. It's been helping, but it's slow going. I'm just eager to get back to my normal activities, like speedcubing and playing board games with my friends.
I can imagine. It's tough to be sidelined when you're used to being active. Do you have any favorite board games that you like to play?
I love playing Scrabble and chess. I'm also really into crossword puzzles. I find them really relaxing and challenging at the same time.
That's great! I've never met anyone who's into speedcubing and crossword puzzles before. How did you get into speedcubing?
I started speedcubing about 10 years ago, when I was 49. I saw a video of someone solving a Rubik's cube in under 10 seconds and I was hooked. I've been practicing ever since.
That's impressive! Do you have any goals for your speedcubing, like competing in tournaments or solving the cube in a certain amount of time?
Actually, I'm thinking of competing in the US Speedcubing Championship next year. I want to solve the cube in under 15 seconds.
That's a great goal! I'm sure you'll get there with practice. Keep me updated on your progress.
